Be truthful (Academic Integrity - Honesty)
Give credit to the owner of the work (i.e., musician, author, artist, speaker etc.) (Academic Integrity - Honesty)
Keep promises (Academic Integrity - Honesty)
Provide factual evidence (Academic Integrity - Honesty)
Aspire to objectivity, consider all sides and one's own potential preconceptions (Academic Integrity - Honesty)
Clearly state expectations and follow through (Academic Integrity - Trust)
Promote transparency in values, processes, and outcomes (Academic Integrity - Trust)
Trust others (Academic Integrity - Trust)
Give credence (Academic Integrity - Trust)
Encourage mutual understanding (Academic Integrity - Trust)
Act with genuineness (Academic Integrity - Trust)
Apply rules and policies consistently (Academic Integrity - Fairness)
Engage with others equitably (Academic Integrity - Fairness)
Keep an open-mind (Academic Integrity - Fairness)
Be objective (Academic Integrity - Fairness)
Take responsibility for your own actions (Academic Integrity - Fairness)
Practice active listening (Academic Integrity - Respect)
Receive feedback willingly (Academic Integrity - Respect)
Accept that others' thoughts and ideas have validity (Academic Integrity - Respect)
Show empathy (Academic Integrity - Respect)
Seek open communication (Academic Integrity - Respect)
Affirm others and accept differences (Academic Integrity - Respect)
Recognize the consequences of our words and actions on others (Academic Integrity - Respect)
Hold yourself accountable for your actions (Academic Integrity - Responsibility)
Engage with others in difficult conversations, even when silence might be easier (Academic Integrity - Responsibility)
Know and follow institutional rules and conduct codes (Academic Integrity - Responsibility)
Create, understand, and respect personal boundaries (Academic Integrity - Responsibility)
Follow through with tasks and expectations (Academic Integrity - Responsibility)
Model good behavior (Academic Integrity - Responsibility)
Be brave even when others might not (Academic Integrity - Courage)
Take a stand to address a wrongdoing and support others doing the same (Academic Integrity - Courage)
Endure discomfort for something you believe in (Academic Integrity - Courage)
Be undaunted in defending integrity (Academic Integrity - Courage)
Be willing to take risk and risk failure (Academic Integrity - Courage)
